title: A lane is a routing destination
canonical_summary: A lane is a stable routing destination for a recurring question class, evidence class, or answer pattern.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Define lane as routing destination and explain when it deserves its own space.
exactness_guard: Do not create lanes just because topic names sound useful.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Lanes need boundaries
canonical_summary: A useful lane has inclusion and exclusion boundaries so the model does not blend neighboring topics.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain that boundaries protect routing quality.
exactness_guard: Do not leave lane scope vague.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Not every idea deserves a lane
canonical_summary: Some ideas belong as cards inside an existing lane rather than becoming separate routing destinations.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Recommend card-first when the idea is a subpattern.
exactness_guard: Do not split lanes without repeated routing need.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Lane kits make lanes testable
canonical_summary: A lane becomes testable when it has overview, concept map, cards, sources, QA, answer patterns, and profile metadata.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: List the lane kit and why each file matters.
exactness_guard: Do not call a title-only folder validated.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Lane is only a title
canonical_summary: A lane with only a title and no cards, sources, or QA prompts cannot train routing behavior.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Mark it as candidate or seed, not validated.
exactness_guard: Do not count empty lanes as training-ready.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Lane overlaps another lane
canonical_summary: Heavy overlap creates routing confusion and should trigger merge, split, or boundary refinement.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Compare boundaries and decide whether to merge or clarify.
exactness_guard: Do not keep duplicate lanes for cosmetic reasons.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Lane has no source coverage
canonical_summary: Cards without source coverage cannot support source-governed RAG.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Flag the gap and require source mapping.
exactness_guard: Do not treat plausible cards as sourced evidence.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: New lane vs new card
canonical_summary: Create a card when the pattern belongs inside an existing route; create a lane when route, evidence, or answer pattern changes.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Ask whether the user question would route differently.
exactness_guard: Do not create lanes for one-off examples.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Merge vs split lanes
canonical_summary: Merge lanes when they duplicate routing; split lanes when one lane contains distinct failure classes or answer patterns.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Use retrieval failures to decide merge/split.
exactness_guard: Do not split or merge only for tidy numbering.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Lane validation source order
canonical_summary: Lane validation schema, pretraining gate, QC reports, and evaluation outputs define readiness.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: State readiness criteria before counting a lane.
exactness_guard: Do not mark a lane validated without required artifacts.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Do not confuse lane count with quality
canonical_summary: The 128-lane target is a structure goal, not a quality guarantee.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain that validation matters more than raw lane count.
exactness_guard: Do not claim more lanes automatically improve the model.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: How to answer lane-design questions
canonical_summary: Answers should identify routing purpose, boundary, source coverage, neighboring lanes, and lane/card decision.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Use route, boundary, source, neighbor, and decision.
exactness_guard: Do not answer with only a name suggestion.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.
